content.problemStatement:
The Chef is on vacation in the beautiful country of Chefville. The island is a narrow strip of land running from north to south. A single road from north to south connects all the villages in the country. There, he observed, the postal services works in a very curious way.




There is a postman for every village. In the morning, say postman A, starts traveling to the North, disbursing all the mails on the way. When he meets another postman coming from the opposite direction on the way, say B, A hands over all the mails to B which are to be delivered to addresses that lie in the North and vice versa for B. Then both turn back and start traveling in the opposite directions. So, now A travels to the South and when he meets another postman on the way, the same thing happens again. This keep on till the duty hours of the day finishes and they don't loose any time in the swapping and turning back. All postmen travel at the same speed of 1 m/s always and they arbitrarily choose their direction to start with in the morning.



Now, at any given time of the day, the Chef wants to know the position of any given postman and the number of times he has met another postman till that time (including this moment of time).
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains four lines. The first line contains N, the number of villages. The second line contains N distinct integers representing the positions of the villages in increasing order. The third line contains the initial directions of the corresponding postmen as N integers, which are either 0 (North) or 1 (South). The fourth line contains the total number of queries (Q) for this test case. The next Q lines contain two integers: the postman number (I) and the time (T) in seconds.
content.outputFormat: For every query of a test case, output the position of the postman number I at time T and the number of times he has met another postman till then, on the same line separated by a space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first query, postman 2 moves north for 11 seconds, meeting another postman twice. For the second query, postman 0 moves south for 9 seconds, not meeting any other postman.
Postman 1 moves north for 2 seconds, meeting another postman once.
Postman 0 moves north for 1 second, meeting another postman once.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by simulating the movement of each postman. For each query, we start the postman at its initial position and move it according to its initial direction. When a postman meets another postman, it changes its direction and continues moving. We keep track of the number of times the postman meets another postman and its final position after the given time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * Q * N * T) per test case, O(T * Q * N * T) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — for storing the positions and directions of the postmen.

code.Python3:
import sys

T = int(input())
while T > 0:
    N = int(input())
    positions = list(map(int, input().split()))
    directions = list(map(int, input().split()))
    Q = int(input())
    while Q > 0:
        I, T = map(int, input().split())
        # Simulate the movement of the postman
        position = positions[I]
        direction = directions[I]
        meets = 0
        for i in range(T):
            if direction == 0:
                # Moving North
                for j in range(I + 1, N):
                    if positions[j] == position + 1:
                        # Meet another postman
                        meets += 1
                        direction = 1
                        break
                if direction == 0:
                    position += 1
            else:
                # Moving South
                for j in range(I - 1, -1, -1):
                    if positions[j] == position - 1:
                        # Meet another postman
                        meets += 1
                        direction = 0
                        break
                if direction == 1:
                    position -= 1
        print(position, meets)
        Q -= 1
    T -= 1
